new_classes.label_en: Rum and other spirits obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.label_de: Rum und anderer Branntwein, gewonnen durch Destillieren vergorener Zuckerrohrerzeugnisse, in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 l (KN 2208 40 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol with alcoholic strength less than 80% vol, being rum or other spirit obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (such as rum and taffia as described in the HS Explanatory Notes to heading 2208, third paragraph, point 3), possessing their typical organoleptic characteristics, and put up in containers holding more than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture all principal discriminating criteria for CN 2208 40 91: (1) the product is an undenatured spirit with alcoholic strength less than 80% vol (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality), (2) obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (FermentedSugarCaneProductsDistillationProcess), (3) possessing the typical organoleptic characteristics of rum/taffia (RumSugarCaneSpiritOrganolepticQuality), and (4) put up in containers of more than 2 litres (LargeContainer, i.e. ≥ 2 L; the TBox LargeContainer is defined as ≥ 2.0 L whereas the CN cut-off for this subheading is > 2 L, a minor boundary nuance not fully representable without a StrictlyLargeContainer class). A disjointness complement with the sibling small-container class (22084051) is included. A small coverage gap remains because the HS Explanatory Note reference (rum and taffia named examples) is purely declaratory and fully captured by the organoleptic quality restriction; also, the LargeContainer class in the TBox uses ≥ 2 L rather than > 2 L, which could in theory include exactly-2 L containers that belong to subheading 22084051 — this is a pre-existing TBox limitation.
